activities.instructions: Choose the correct word for a reported yes/no question.
activities.prompt: “Is the shop open?” He asked ___ the shop was open.

options.text: if
that
to
what

activities.explanation: Una pregunta sí/no se introduce con «if» o «whether».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct reported question order.
activities.prompt: “Where is the station?” She asked ___.

options.text: where the station was
where was the station
where the station is?
that where the station was

activities.explanation: La pregunta reportada usa orden afirmativo.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct structure for an instruction.
activities.prompt: “Wait outside,” the guard said to us. The guard ___ outside.

options.text: told us to wait
told to us wait
said us to wait
asked if we wait

activities.explanation: Una instrucción usa «told + persona + to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct structure for a polite request.
activities.prompt: “Please help me,” Maya said to Leo. Maya ___.

options.text: asked Leo to help her
told Leo if he helped her
asked to Leo help her
said Leo to help me

activities.explanation: Una petición usa «asked + persona + to» y adapta el pronombre.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct negative command structure.
activities.prompt: “Don't touch the switch,” she told me. She told me ___.

options.text: not to touch the switch
to not touched the switch
don't touch the switch
if I didn't touch the switch

activities.explanation: La orden negativa usa «not to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct reported yes/no question.
activities.prompt: “Do you need a receipt?” the cashier asked me.

options.text: The cashier asked me if I needed a receipt.
The cashier asked me did I need a receipt.
The cashier told me if I needed a receipt.
The cashier asked that I need a receipt.

activities.explanation: Se elimina «do» y se usa orden afirmativo tras «if».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct reported wh-question.
activities.prompt: “Why is the gate locked?” Nina asked.

options.text: Nina asked why the gate was locked.
Nina asked why was the gate locked.
Nina asked if why the gate was locked.
Nina told why the gate is locked.

activities.explanation: Se conserva «why», se usa orden afirmativo y se aplica backshift.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report of an instruction.
activities.prompt: “Keep the receipt,” the assistant said to me.

options.text: The assistant told me to keep the receipt.
The assistant asked me if I kept the receipt.
The assistant told to keep me the receipt.
The assistant said me to keep the receipt.

activities.explanation: La instrucción usa «told me to keep».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report of a polite request.
activities.prompt: “Please speak more slowly,” I said to the guide.

options.text: I asked the guide to speak more slowly.
I told the guide if she spoke slowly.
I asked to the guide speak slowly.
I said the guide to speak more slowly.

activities.explanation: «Please» indica una petición con «asked + objeto + to».

activities.instructions: Choose the structure that reports a question about ability.
activities.prompt: “Can you swim?” she asked me.

options.text: She asked me if I could swim.
She asked me to swim.
She told me if I can swim.
She asked could I swim.

activities.explanation: Pregunta por capacidad, por eso usa «asked if», no «asked to».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct past reported question.
activities.prompt: “When did the meeting start?” they asked.

options.text: They asked when the meeting had started.
They asked when did the meeting start.
They asked if when the meeting started.
They told when had the meeting started.

activities.explanation: Se elimina «did», se usa orden afirmativo y past perfect.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct reported present perfect question.
activities.prompt: “Has the parcel arrived?” Jo asked.

options.text: Jo asked whether the parcel had arrived.
Jo asked whether had the parcel arrived.
Jo told if the parcel has arrived.
Jo asked that the parcel had arrive.

activities.explanation: Present perfect retrocede a past perfect tras «whether».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report of a negative instruction.
activities.prompt: “Don't block the entrance,” the marshal told the drivers.

options.text: The marshal told the drivers not to block the entrance.
The marshal asked if the drivers didn't block the entrance.
The marshal told the drivers don't block the entrance.
The marshal said the drivers to not blocked the entrance.

activities.explanation: La instrucción negativa usa «told + objeto + not to».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report of a negative request.
activities.prompt: “Please don't mention my address,” Maya said to me.

options.text: Maya asked me not to mention her address.
Maya told me if I didn't mention my address.
Maya asked me to not mentioned her address.
Maya said me not mention her address.

activities.explanation: La petición negativa usa «asked me not to» y cambia el posesivo.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct structure for a polite action request.
activities.prompt: “Could you open the window, please?” Sara said to Leo.

options.text: Sara asked Leo to open the window.
Sara asked Leo if he could open the window.
Sara told Leo whether he opened the window.
Sara asked to Leo opening the window.

activities.explanation: En contexto, «Could you ... please?» es una petición de acción.

activities.instructions: Choose the accurate report made several days later.
activities.prompt: “Will you be here tomorrow?” Nina asked Omar.

options.text: Nina asked Omar whether he would be there the next day.
Nina asked Omar will he be here tomorrow.
Nina told Omar if he would be there next day.
Nina asked whether would Omar be here the day before.

activities.explanation: Cambian orden, «will», pronombre, lugar y tiempo.

activities.instructions: Choose the accurate reported wh-question.
activities.prompt: “Where did you put these keys?” Amir asked Lena.

options.text: Amir asked Lena where she had put those keys.
Amir asked Lena where did she put these keys.
Amir told Lena where she has put those keys.
Amir asked if where Lena had put this keys.

activities.explanation: Se eliminan «did» e inversión y cambian pronombre, tiempo y demostrativo.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report when “who” is the subject.
activities.prompt: “Who called you?” Eva asked me.

options.text: Eva asked who had called me.
Eva asked who did call me.
Eva asked who had I called.
Eva told me who called you?

activities.explanation: «Who» ya es sujeto; no añadimos «did» ni invertimos.

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence reporting a question, not a request.
activities.prompt: The manager wanted to know whether I was available to assist.

options.text: The manager asked me if I could help.
The manager asked me to help.
The manager told me to help.
The manager asked that I help him now.

activities.explanation: «Asked me if» solicita información sobre disponibilidad; «asked me to» pediría la acción.

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence reporting a request, not a question.
activities.prompt: The manager wanted me to perform the action.

options.text: The manager asked me to help.
The manager asked me if I could help.
The manager asked whether I was able to help.
The manager asked if to help.

activities.explanation: «Asked me to help» es una petición de acción.

activities.instructions: Choose the only fully accurate report made the following week.
activities.prompt: On Monday Rina asked Leo, “Can you bring these folders here tomorrow?”

options.text: Rina asked Leo to bring those folders there the next day.
Rina asked Leo if he could bring these folders here tomorrow.
Rina told Leo whether he can brought those folders there.
Rina asked to Leo bring that folders the day before.

activities.explanation: La forma cortés solicita una acción y cambian demostrativo, lugar y tiempo.

activities.instructions: Choose the only fully accurate report.
activities.prompt: “Why haven't you sent me the revised figures?” Nora asked Sam.

options.text: Nora asked Sam why he hadn't sent her the revised figures.
Nora asked Sam why hadn't he sent me the revised figures.
Nora told Sam why he hasn't send her the figures.
Nora asked if why Sam didn't sent her the figures.

activities.explanation: Se usa orden afirmativo, past perfect y pronombres adaptados.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct pair of reported actions.
activities.prompt: The guide said, “Stay together, and don't cross the rope.”

options.text: The guide told us to stay together and not to cross the rope.
The guide asked if we stayed together and didn't cross the rope.
The guide told to us stay together and don't crossing the rope.
The guide said us to stay and not cross the rope.

activities.explanation: Las instrucciones afirmativa y negativa comparten «told us».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct formal alternative with “whether or not”.
activities.prompt: “Is the fee refundable?” the customer asked.

options.text: The customer asked whether or not the fee was refundable.
The customer asked whether was or not the fee refundable.
The customer told if the fee had refundable or not.
The customer asked that the fee is refundable or not.

activities.explanation: «Whether or not» va seguido de orden afirmativo.

activities.instructions: Choose the report that correctly distinguishes question and request.
activities.prompt: First Ana said, “Can you drive?” Then she said, “Could you drive me home, please?”

options.text: Ana asked me if I could drive, then asked me to drive her home.
Ana asked me to drive, then asked if I could drive her home.
Ana told me whether I drove, then asked me if to drive her home.
Ana asked could I drive, then told to me driving her home.

activities.explanation: La primera frase pregunta capacidad; la segunda solicita una acción.
